title: malus communis subsp. mitis
intro: Malus communis subsp. mitis, also known as the wild apple or crab apple, is a subspecies of apple tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Malus communis subsp. mitis plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of this plant have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Malus communis subsp. mitis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety should be further studied.
